compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Why

MCP tools carry no behavior annotations, so clients cannot present safe UI or
confirmation flows. rebar already has the semantic information internally —
reads are separate from writes, writes are hidden under `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`,
Jira-live and LLM tools are call-gated — but none of it is exposed as the MCP
`ToolAnnotations` hints (`readOnlyHint`, `destructiveHint`, `idempotentHint`,
`openWorldHint`) that the spec defines for exactly this purpose.

## What

Annotate every registered tool via `FastMCP.tool(annotations=ToolAnnotations(...))`
across the three registrars (`register_read_tools` in `_mcp_reads.py`,
`register_llm_tools` in `_mcp_llm.py`, `register_write_tools` in `_mcp_writes.py`).
Confirmed feasible: `mcp` 1.28.1 exposes `ToolAnnotations` with all four hint
fields and `FastMCP.tool` accepts `annotations`.

Mapping policy:

- **Read tools** -> `readOnlyHint: true`.
- **Additive writes** (create/comment/tag/link/edit/claim/transition/set_*) ->
  `readOnlyHint: false`, `destructiveHint: false`; `idempotentHint: true` where
  the op is idempotent (e.g. tag/untag).
- **Destructive/irreversible** (archive/delete/compact, reconcile-live) ->
  `destructiveHint: true`.
- **Outward-reaching** (LLM review/verify, Jira reconcile, `run_workflow`) ->
  `openWorldHint: true`.

## Scope

`src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py`, `_mcp_llm.py`, `_mcp_writes.py`. Annotations are hints
only — no behavior change. Add/adjust a parity test if the interface suite pins
tool metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every `@mcp.tool()` in the three registrars carries a `ToolAnnotations`
  consistent with the mapping policy above.
- [ ] Read tools are `readOnlyHint: true`; delete/archive/reconcile-live are
  `destructiveHint: true`; LLM/Jira/workflow tools are `openWorldHint: true`.
- [ ] The server still builds and starts; existing MCP/interface tests pass.
- [ ] No functional behavior of any tool changes.
compiled_state.title: Annotate MCP tools with behavior hints (read-only/destructive/idempotent/open-world)
